description_long: This is a round ceramic bowl with a shallow profile and a wide opening, featuring a striking radial pattern on its interior. The pattern consists of alternating blue and brown brushstroke lines radiating from the center to the rim, set against a white background. The exterior of the bowl is mostly white with some visible blue and brown streaks, and there are a few small chips and imperfections along the rim and body. The base is slightly raised and unglazed, showing a natural ceramic color. The bowl's surface appears to be matte with a slightly rough texture, typical of hand-painted pottery. This type of bowl is often found in traditional or rustic kitchen settings and may be used for serving or display.
description: A round ceramic bowl with blue and brown radial brushstroke patterns on a white background, showing some chips and a matte finish.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full radial blue and brown pattern inside the bowl.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the bowl's depth and the continuation of the pattern onto the inner walls.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view revealing the unglazed base and some exterior streaks, as well as chips and imperfections.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, again showing the bowl's depth and the hand-painted pattern inside.
attribute_context_of_use: serving food or holding small items
attribute_decorative_patterns: Alternating blue and brown radial brushstroke lines on the interior surface, with some streaks on the exterior.
